---
title: "Perry_model_boostrap_05_12_2026"
format: html
---


```{r setup}
library(beepr)
source("../code/modeling_functions_05_02_2026.R")
source("../code/pedon_pack_functions_05_02_2026.R")


```

```{r load-data}
# ============================================================
# LOAD PERRY PACK + CLEANED HORIZON DATA + BUNDLES
# ============================================================

# --- Most recent Perry pack ---
perry_pack_files <- list.files("../outputs",
                               pattern = "^perry_pack_150_.*\\.csv$",
                               full.names = TRUE)
perry_pack_path <- perry_pack_files[which.max(file.mtime(perry_pack_files))]
cat("Loading Perry pack:", perry_pack_path, "\n")

perry_pack_150 <- read.csv(perry_pack_path) %>%
  mutate(
    peiid = as.character(peiid),
    POINT_X = as.numeric(POINT_X),  # force numeric (NA -> NA_real_)
    POINT_Y = as.numeric(POINT_Y)
  )

# --- Most recent cleaned Perry FP (for truth labels) ---
perry_fp_files <- list.files("../outputs",
                             pattern = "^perry_fp_clean_.*\\.csv$",
                             full.names = TRUE)
perry_fp_path <- perry_fp_files[which.max(file.mtime(perry_fp_files))]
cat("Loading Perry FP clean:", perry_fp_path, "\n")

perry_fp_clean <- read.csv(perry_fp_path) %>%
  mutate(peiid = as.character(Point))

# # --- All available NCSS bundles ---
# bundle_files <- list.files("../outputs/models_05_02",
#                            pattern = "_bundle\\.rds$",
#                            full.names = TRUE)
# 
# # Filter out the legacy taxsubgrp bundle (different outcome name from taxsubgrp_mod1).
# bundle_files <- bundle_files[
#   basename(bundle_files) != "taxsubgrp_xgb_plain_bundle.rds"
# ]
# 
# cat("Found", length(bundle_files), "bundles\n")

bundle_files <- list.files("../outputs/models_05_02",
                           pattern = "_bundle\\.rds$",
                           full.names = TRUE)

# Exclude legacy taxsubgrp and replicate-tagged bundles
bundle_files <- bundle_files[
  basename(bundle_files) != "taxsubgrp_xgb_plain_bundle.rds" &
  !str_detect(basename(bundle_files), "_rep[0-9]+_bundle\\.rds$")
]

cat("Found", length(bundle_files), "canonical bundles\n")

bundles <- map(bundle_files, readRDS) %>%
  set_names(map_chr(., "name"))

# --- Reattach UMAP fits where needed ---
bundles <- map(bundles, function(b) {
  if ((b$model_type %||% "") == "umap_hdb") {
    raw_path <- b$umap_model_file %||% ""
    candidate_paths <- unique(c(
      raw_path,
      sub("^\\.\\./", "", raw_path),
      file.path(getwd(), raw_path),
      file.path(getwd(), sub("^\\.\\./", "", raw_path))
    ))
    candidate_paths <- candidate_paths[file.exists(candidate_paths)]
    if (length(candidate_paths) == 0) {
      warning("UMAP file not found for ", b$name, " (was: ", raw_path, ")")
      return(b)
    }
    b$umap_fit <- uwot::load_uwot(candidate_paths[1])
  }
  b
})
```

```{r bundle-inventory}
# ============================================================
# BUNDLE INVENTORY
# ============================================================

bundle_inventory <- map_dfr(bundles, function(b) {
  tibble(
    name        = b$name,
    outcome     = b$outcome,
    model_type  = b$model_type,
    has_umap    = !is.null(b$umap_fit),
    n_train     = nrow(b$train_raw),
    n_classes   = if (!is.null(b$train_raw[[b$outcome]])) {
      length(unique(b$train_raw[[b$outcome]]))
    } else NA_integer_,
    test_kap    = b$test_metrics$kap %||% NA_real_,
    test_bal_acc= b$test_metrics$bal_accuracy %||% NA_real_,
    test_topk   = b$test_metrics$top_k %||% NA_real_
  )
})

cat("\n--- Bundle inventory ---\n")
print(bundle_inventory, n = Inf)

write.csv(bundle_inventory,
          "../outputs/reports_05_02/perry_bundle_inventory.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r domain-shift}
# ============================================================
# DOMAIN-SHIFT DIAGNOSTIC
# Train a binary classifier (NCSS vs Perry) on baked features.
# Runs once, using the taxorder rf_plain bundle's recipe.
# Interpretation:
#   acc < 0.6  : low shift (good)
#   0.6 - 0.75 : moderate shift
#   > 0.75     : strong shift; predictions need care
# ============================================================

ds_bundle <- bundles$taxorder_rf_plain

if (!is.null(ds_bundle)) {

  ncss_baked <- bake(ds_bundle$prep_rec, new_data = ds_bundle$train_raw) %>%
    select(-any_of(c(ds_bundle$outcome, "peiid"))) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.logical), as.double)) %>%
    select(where(is.numeric))

  perry_padded <- pad_like_train_raw(perry_pack_150, ds_bundle$train_raw,
                                     ds_bundle$outcome)
  perry_baked <- bake(ds_bundle$prep_rec, new_data = perry_padded) %>%
    select(-any_of(c(ds_bundle$outcome, "peiid"))) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.logical), as.double)) %>%
    select(where(is.numeric))

  ds_result <- domain_shift_check(ncss_baked, perry_baked, seed = 42)

  cat("\n========================================\n")
  cat("DOMAIN-SHIFT DIAGNOSTIC\n")
  cat("========================================\n")
  cat("Binary classifier (NCSS vs Perry) accuracy:",
      round(ds_result$accuracy, 3), "\n")
  cat("Interpretation:", ds_result$interpretation, "\n")
  cat("\nTop features distinguishing NCSS from Perry:\n")
  print(ds_result$top_features)

  saveRDS(ds_result, "../outputs/reports_05_02/domain_shift_result.rds")
} else {
  message("No taxorder_rf_plain bundle found — skipping domain shift check.")
  ds_result <- NULL
}
```

```{r build-truth}
# ============================================================
# BUILD PERRY PER-PEDON TRUTH LABELS
# ============================================================

build_perry_truth <- function(perry_fp_clean, truth_col) {
  truth_col <- rlang::as_name(rlang::ensym(truth_col))

  if (!truth_col %in% names(perry_fp_clean)) {
    return(tibble(peiid = character(), truth = character(),
                  n_truth_unique = integer()))
  }

  dat <- perry_fp_clean %>%
    mutate(truth_val = na_if(str_squish(as.character(.data[[truth_col]])), ""))

  # Match NCSS conventions
  if (truth_col == "taxpartsize") {
    dat <- dat %>% mutate(truth_val = str_to_lower(truth_val))
  }
  if (truth_col == "series_clean") {
    dat <- dat %>% mutate(truth_val = na_if(truth_val, "Fill")) %>%
                   mutate(truth_val = na_if(truth_val, "fill")) %>%
                   mutate(truth_val = str_to_title(truth_val))
  }

  dat %>%
    group_by(peiid) %>%
    summarise(
      n_hz = n(),
      n_truth_nonNA = sum(!is.na(truth_val)),
      n_truth_unique = n_distinct(truth_val[!is.na(truth_val)]),
      truth = {
        nv <- truth_val[!is.na(truth_val)]
        if (length(nv) == 0) NA_character_ else
          names(sort(table(nv), decreasing = TRUE))[1]
      },
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}

# Map outcome name -> truth column in perry_fp_clean
truth_col_map <- c(
  taxorder        = "taxorder",
  taxsuborder     = "taxsuborder",
  taxgrtgroup     = "taxgrtgroup",
  taxsubgrp_mod1  = "taxsubgrp_mod1",
  taxpartsize     = "taxpartsize",
  taxonname_clean = "series_clean"
)

perry_truth <- map(truth_col_map, ~ build_perry_truth(perry_fp_clean, !!sym(.x))) %>%
  set_names(names(truth_col_map))

cat("\n--- Perry truth coverage ---\n")
walk2(perry_truth, names(perry_truth), function(t, nm) {
  n_with_truth <- sum(!is.na(t$truth) & t$n_truth_unique == 1)
  cat(nm, ": ", n_with_truth, " / ", nrow(t),
      " pedons with unambiguous truth\n", sep = "")
})
```

```{r deploy}
# ============================================================
# DEPLOY EVERY BUNDLE TO PERRY
# ============================================================

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("DEPLOYING BUNDLES TO PERRY\n")
cat("========================================\n")

perry_preds <- map(bundles, function(b) {
  cat("  -- ", b$name, "\n")
  tryCatch(
    deploy_bundle_to_new(b, perry_pack_150) %>%
      mutate(peiid = as.character(peiid)),
    error = function(e) {
      cat("     ERROR:", e$message, "\n")
      NULL
    }
  )
})

# Drop bundles that failed
perry_preds <- compact(perry_preds)

# Long-format predictions table for export
perry_preds_long <- map_dfr(perry_preds, function(pr) {
  pr %>%
    select(peiid, model_name, outcome, model_type, .pred_class, top1, margin)
})

cat("\nTotal pedon-by-model predictions: ", nrow(perry_preds_long), "\n")

write.csv(perry_preds_long,
          "../outputs/reports_05_02/perry_predictions_long.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r validate}
# ============================================================
# VALIDATE PREDICTIONS AGAINST PERRY TRUTH
# ============================================================

validate_one_bundle <- function(pred_df, truth_df, outcome) {
  pred_df  <- pred_df  %>% mutate(peiid = as.character(peiid))
  truth_df <- truth_df %>% mutate(peiid = as.character(peiid))

  joined <- pred_df %>%
    left_join(truth_df %>% select(peiid, truth, n_truth_unique), by = "peiid") %>%
    filter(!is.na(truth), n_truth_unique == 1)

  if (nrow(joined) < 2) {
    return(list(data = joined, metrics = NULL, conf_mat = NULL,
                n_pedons = nrow(joined)))
  }

  # Union of all classes seen in truth, predictions, and bundle's training set
  # (the prob columns tell us what the model knows about).
  prob_cols <- names(joined)[
    str_detect(names(joined), "^\\.pred_") & names(joined) != ".pred_class"
  ]
  trained_classes <- str_remove(prob_cols, "^\\.pred_")

  lvl <- union(union(
    as.character(unique(joined$truth)),
    as.character(unique(joined$.pred_class))
  ), trained_classes)

  joined <- joined %>%
    mutate(
      truth_fct   = factor(truth, levels = lvl),
      pred_fct    = factor(as.character(.pred_class), levels = lvl),
      .pred_class = pred_fct  # overwrite so downstream metric calls also see matched levels
    )

  # All metrics computed on the harmonized factors
  k_for_topk <- if (outcome == "taxonname_clean") 5 else 3

# Replace the existing 'mets <- tibble(...)' block in validate_one_bundle with:

# Only score classes actually present in Perry truth
present_in_truth <- as.character(unique(joined$truth_fct))
joined_present <- joined %>%
  filter(as.character(truth_fct) %in% present_in_truth) %>%
  mutate(
    truth_fct = factor(as.character(truth_fct), levels = present_in_truth),
    pred_fct  = factor(
      as.character(pred_fct),
      levels = union(present_in_truth, as.character(unique(pred_fct)))
    )
  )

# For bal_accuracy/f_meas, restrict to a "fair" comparison universe:
# truth-present classes plus any class actually predicted.
fair_lvls <- union(present_in_truth, as.character(unique(joined_present$pred_fct)))
joined_fair <- joined_present %>%
  mutate(
    truth_fct = factor(as.character(truth_fct), levels = fair_lvls),
    pred_fct  = factor(as.character(pred_fct),  levels = fair_lvls)
  )

k_for_topk <- if (outcome == "taxonname_clean") 5 else 3

mets <- tibble(
  accuracy     = accuracy(joined, truth = truth_fct, estimate = pred_fct)$.estimate,
  kap          = kap(joined,      truth = truth_fct, estimate = pred_fct)$.estimate,
  bal_accuracy = suppressWarnings(
    bal_accuracy(joined_fair, truth = truth_fct, estimate = pred_fct)$.estimate
  ),
  f_meas_macro = suppressWarnings(
    f_meas(joined_fair, truth = truth_fct, estimate = pred_fct,
           estimator = "macro")$.estimate
  ),
  top_k        = top_k_accuracy(joined, "truth_fct", k = k_for_topk),
  k            = k_for_topk,
  n_truth_classes      = length(present_in_truth),
  n_predicted_classes  = length(unique(as.character(joined$pred_fct))),
  n_trained_classes    = length(unique(c(present_in_truth,
                                          str_remove(prob_cols, "^\\.pred_"))))
)

  cm <- conf_mat(joined, truth = truth_fct, estimate = pred_fct)

  list(
    data     = joined,
    metrics  = mets,
    conf_mat = cm,
    n_pedons = nrow(joined)
  )
}

perry_validations <- list()

for (nm in names(perry_preds)) {
  b <- bundles[[nm]]
  oc <- b$outcome

  # series bundles use "taxonname_clean" outcome but truth from "series_clean"
  truth_key <- oc

  truth_df <- perry_truth[[truth_key]]
  if (is.null(truth_df) || nrow(truth_df) == 0) {
    cat(nm, ": no truth available (skipping)\n")
    next
  }

  v <- validate_one_bundle(perry_preds[[nm]], truth_df, oc)
  perry_validations[[nm]] <- v
}

# ---- Summary ----
perry_metric_summary <- map_dfr(perry_validations, function(v) {
  if (is.null(v$metrics)) return(NULL)
  v$metrics %>% mutate(n_pedons = v$n_pedons)
}, .id = "bundle")

# Add outcome column for grouping
perry_metric_summary <- perry_metric_summary %>%
  mutate(
    outcome = bundle %>%
      str_remove("_rf_plain$|_xgb_plain$|_rf_umap_hdb$|_xgb_umap_hdb$") %>%
      str_remove("_min5$"),
    is_min5 = str_detect(bundle, "_min5_"),
    engine = case_when(
      str_detect(bundle, "_rf_")  ~ "rf",
      str_detect(bundle, "_xgb_") ~ "xgb"
    ),
    space = case_when(
      str_detect(bundle, "_umap_hdb$") ~ "umap_hdb",
      TRUE                              ~ "plain"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(outcome, engine, space, is_min5, bundle, n_pedons,
         accuracy, kap, bal_accuracy, f_meas_macro, top_k, k)

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("PERRY EXTERNAL VALIDATION SUMMARY\n")
cat("========================================\n")
print(perry_metric_summary %>% arrange(outcome, desc(kap)), n = Inf)

write.csv(perry_metric_summary,
          "../outputs/reports_05_02/perry_external_validation.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)
```



```{r per-class-and-confusion}
# ============================================================
# PER-CLASS ACCURACY + CONFUSION PAIRS
# ============================================================

per_class_acc <- function(joined_df) {
  joined_df %>%
    mutate(across(c(truth_fct, pred_fct), as.character)) %>%
    group_by(truth_fct) %>%
    summarise(
      n         = n(),
      n_correct = sum(truth_fct == pred_fct),
      acc       = round(n_correct / n, 3),
      .groups   = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(n))
}

confusion_pairs <- function(joined_df) {
  joined_df %>%
    mutate(across(c(truth_fct, pred_fct), as.character)) %>%
    filter(truth_fct != pred_fct) %>%
    count(truth_fct, pred_fct, sort = TRUE)
}

# Pick best Perry-side bundle per outcome (by kap), then dump details
best_perry_bundle <- perry_metric_summary %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  slice_max(kap, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("BEST PERRY-SIDE BUNDLE PER OUTCOME (by kap)\n")
cat("========================================\n")
print(best_perry_bundle)

for (i in seq_len(nrow(best_perry_bundle))) {
  bn <- best_perry_bundle$bundle[i]
  oc <- best_perry_bundle$outcome[i]

  v <- perry_validations[[bn]]
  if (is.null(v) || is.null(v$data)) next

  cat("\n----- ", bn, " (", oc, ") -----\n", sep = "")

  cat("\nPer-class:\n")
  print(per_class_acc(v$data))

  cat("\nTop misclassification pairs:\n")
  cp <- confusion_pairs(v$data)
  if (nrow(cp) > 0) print(cp %>% slice_head(n = 10)) else cat("(none)\n")
}
```

```{r hierarchical-accuracy}
# ============================================================
# HIERARCHICAL-PARENT ACCURACY
# "When the child outcome was wrong, was the parent outcome right?"
# Useful for: subgroup_mod1 (parent=great group),
#             great group (parent=suborder),
#             suborder (parent=order),
#             series (parent=subgroup_mod1).
# ============================================================

# Use the BEST Perry-side bundle per outcome (already computed)
best_per_outcome <- best_perry_bundle %>%
  select(outcome, bundle)

perry_pred_wide <- perry_preds_long %>%
  filter(model_name %in% best_per_outcome$bundle) %>%
  mutate(.pred_class = as.character(.pred_class)) %>%
  # Force one row per (peiid, outcome) - take the first if duplicates
  arrange(peiid, outcome) %>%
  distinct(peiid, outcome, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  select(peiid, outcome, .pred_class) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols = peiid,
    names_from = outcome,
    values_from = .pred_class,
    names_prefix = "pred_",
    values_fill = NA_character_
  )

# Verify column types
cat("Column types after pivot_wider:\n")
print(sapply(perry_pred_wide, class))

# Wide table: peiid x outcome -> truth class
perry_truth_wide <- imap_dfr(perry_truth, function(t, nm) {
  if (nrow(t) == 0) return(NULL)
  t %>% mutate(outcome = nm) %>%
    filter(!is.na(truth), n_truth_unique == 1) %>%
    select(peiid, outcome, truth)
}) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = outcome, values_from = truth,
              names_prefix = "truth_")

# Join
hier <- perry_pred_wide %>%
  left_join(perry_truth_wide, by = "peiid")

# Compute parent-correct given child-incorrect
hier_pairs <- list(
  list(child = "taxonname_clean", parent = "taxsubgrp_mod1"),
  list(child = "taxsubgrp_mod1",  parent = "taxgrtgroup"),
  list(child = "taxgrtgroup",     parent = "taxsuborder"),
  list(child = "taxsuborder",     parent = "taxorder")
)

hier_acc <- map_dfr(hier_pairs, function(pr) {
  pred_c <- paste0("pred_",  pr$child)
  truth_c <- paste0("truth_", pr$child)
  pred_p <- paste0("pred_",  pr$parent)
  truth_p <- paste0("truth_", pr$parent)

  needed <- c(pred_c, truth_c, pred_p, truth_p)
  if (!all(needed %in% names(hier))) return(NULL)

  d <- hier %>%
    select(all_of(needed)) %>%
    rename(pred_child = !!pred_c, truth_child = !!truth_c,
           pred_parent = !!pred_p, truth_parent = !!truth_p) %>%
    filter(!is.na(truth_child), !is.na(truth_parent))

  if (nrow(d) == 0) return(NULL)

  d <- d %>%
    mutate(
      child_correct  = as.character(pred_child)  == as.character(truth_child),
      parent_correct = as.character(pred_parent) == as.character(truth_parent)
    )
tibble(
    child = pr$child,
    parent = pr$parent,
    n = nrow(d),
    child_acc  = mean(d$child_correct,  na.rm = TRUE),
    parent_acc = mean(d$parent_correct, na.rm = TRUE),
    parent_acc_when_child_wrong =
      if (any(!d$child_correct, na.rm = TRUE))
        mean(d$parent_correct[!d$child_correct], na.rm = TRUE)
      else NA_real_,
    both_correct = mean(d$child_correct & d$parent_correct, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
})

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("HIERARCHICAL-PARENT ACCURACY\n")
cat("Read 'parent_acc_when_child_wrong' as:\n")
cat("  When the model misses the child class, how often does it\n")
cat("  still get the parent (less specific) class right?\n")
cat("========================================\n")
print(hier_acc)

write.csv(hier_acc,
          "../outputs/reports_05_02/perry_hierarchical_accuracy.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)
```


```{r rhodic-diagnostic}

# ============================================================
# RHODIC-SPECIFIC FAILURE ANALYSIS
# Why are 0/40 true Rhodic pedons predicted as Rhodic?
# Was Rhodic the close runner-up, or completely absent from
# the model's consideration?
# ============================================================

# 1) Get the best Perry-side subgroup_mod1 bundle and its predictions
sub_bundle_name <- best_perry_bundle %>%
  filter(outcome == "taxsubgrp_mod1") %>%
  pull(bundle)

if (length(sub_bundle_name) == 0) {
  message("No taxsubgrp_mod1 bundle for Rhodic diagnostic.")
} else {

  sub_pred <- perry_preds[[sub_bundle_name]]
  sub_truth <- perry_truth$taxsubgrp_mod1

  # 2) Join with truth, keep only true Rhodic pedons
  rhodic_pedons <- sub_pred %>%
    mutate(peiid = as.character(peiid)) %>%
    left_join(
      sub_truth %>% mutate(peiid = as.character(peiid)) %>%
        select(peiid, truth, n_truth_unique),
      by = "peiid"
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(truth), n_truth_unique == 1, truth == "Rhodic")

  cat("\n========================================\n")
  cat("RHODIC FAILURE DIAGNOSTIC\n")
  cat("Bundle:", sub_bundle_name, "\n")
  cat("True Rhodic pedons:", nrow(rhodic_pedons), "\n")
  cat("========================================\n")

  # 3) Where did Rhodic rank in the predicted probabilities?
  prob_cols <- names(rhodic_pedons)[
    str_detect(names(rhodic_pedons), "^\\.pred_") &
      names(rhodic_pedons) != ".pred_class"
  ]
  class_names <- str_remove(prob_cols, "^\\.pred_")
  
  rhodic_rank_summary <- map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(rhodic_pedons)), function(i) {
    probs <- as.numeric(rhodic_pedons[i, prob_cols])
    names(probs) <- class_names
  
    sorted <- sort(probs, decreasing = TRUE)
  
    # GUARD: if Rhodic isn't in the model's classes, return NA gracefully
    rhodic_prob <- if ("Rhodic" %in% names(probs)) probs[["Rhodic"]] else NA_real_
    rhodic_rank <- if ("Rhodic" %in% names(probs)) which(names(sorted) == "Rhodic") else integer(0)
  
    tibble(
      peiid           = rhodic_pedons$peiid[i],
      predicted_class = as.character(rhodic_pedons$.pred_class[i]),
      pred_top1_prob  = sorted[1],
      pred_top1_class = names(sorted)[1],
      rhodic_prob     = rhodic_prob,
      rhodic_rank     = if (length(rhodic_rank) > 0) rhodic_rank else NA_integer_,
      margin_top1_minus_rhodic = sorted[1] - (rhodic_prob %||% NA_real_)
    )
  })

  cat("\n--- Where did Rhodic rank? ---\n")
  rhodic_rank_summary %>%
    count(rhodic_rank, name = "n_pedons") %>%
    mutate(rank_label = case_when(
      rhodic_rank == 1 ~ "1st (correct)",
      rhodic_rank == 2 ~ "2nd (close miss)",
      rhodic_rank == 3 ~ "3rd",
      rhodic_rank == 4 ~ "4th",
      rhodic_rank >= 5 ~ "5th+",
      TRUE ~ "unknown"
    )) %>%
    select(rank_label, n_pedons) %>%
    print()

  cat("\n--- Distribution of Rhodic probability when wrong ---\n")
  print(summary(rhodic_rank_summary$rhodic_prob))

  cat("\n--- Confidence in Typic when truth is Rhodic ---\n")
  rhodic_rank_summary %>%
    filter(predicted_class == "Typic") %>%
    summarise(
      n               = n(),
      mean_typic_prob = round(mean(pred_top1_prob, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
      mean_rhodic_prob= round(mean(rhodic_prob,    na.rm = TRUE), 3),
      mean_margin     = round(mean(margin_top1_minus_rhodic, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
      n_high_conf     = sum(pred_top1_prob > 0.7, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_low_conf      = sum(pred_top1_prob < 0.5, na.rm = TRUE)
    ) %>%
    print()

  # 4) Compare features: Perry's true Rhodics vs NCSS's true Rhodics
  ncss_rhodic <- bundles[[sub_bundle_name]]$train_raw %>%
    filter(taxsubgrp_mod1 == "Rhodic") %>%
    select(any_of(c("R_w", "G_w", "B_w", "clay_w", "sand_w", "silt_w",
                    "depth_to_Bt_cm", "bt_thickness_cm",
                    "max_clay_ratio_0_150", "max_clay_delta_0_150")))

  perry_rhodic <- perry_pack_150 %>%
    filter(peiid %in% rhodic_pedons$peiid) %>%
    select(any_of(names(ncss_rhodic)))

  cat("\n--- Feature comparison: NCSS Rhodics vs Perry Rhodics ---\n")
  feat_compare <- map_dfr(names(ncss_rhodic), function(nm) {
    n_vals <- ncss_rhodic[[nm]]
    p_vals <- perry_rhodic[[nm]]
    tibble(
      feature       = nm,
      ncss_n        = sum(!is.na(n_vals)),
      perry_n       = sum(!is.na(p_vals)),
      ncss_median   = round(median(n_vals,  na.rm = TRUE), 3),
      perry_median  = round(median(p_vals,  na.rm = TRUE), 3),
      ncss_iqr      = round(IQR(n_vals,     na.rm = TRUE), 3),
      perry_iqr     = round(IQR(p_vals,     na.rm = TRUE), 3),
      median_shift  = round(median(p_vals, na.rm = TRUE) -
                              median(n_vals, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
    )
  }) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(median_shift)))

  print(feat_compare)

  # 5) For comparison: Perry's NON-Rhodic (Typic) pedons - are Perry Rhodics
  #    closer to NCSS Rhodics or to Perry Typics?
  perry_typic <- perry_pack_150 %>%
    inner_join(
      sub_truth %>% mutate(peiid = as.character(peiid)) %>%
        filter(truth == "Typic", n_truth_unique == 1) %>%
        select(peiid),
      by = "peiid"
    ) %>%
    select(any_of(names(ncss_rhodic)))

  cat("\n--- Three-way: NCSS Rhodics, Perry Rhodics, Perry Typics (medians) ---\n")
  three_way <- map_dfr(names(ncss_rhodic), function(nm) {
    tibble(
      feature           = nm,
      ncss_rhodic_med   = round(median(ncss_rhodic[[nm]],  na.rm = TRUE), 3),
      perry_rhodic_med  = round(median(perry_rhodic[[nm]], na.rm = TRUE), 3),
      perry_typic_med   = round(median(perry_typic[[nm]],  na.rm = TRUE), 3)
    ) %>%
      mutate(
        # Is Perry Rhodic closer to NCSS Rhodic or to Perry Typic?
        dist_to_ncss_rhodic = abs(perry_rhodic_med - ncss_rhodic_med),
        dist_to_perry_typic = abs(perry_rhodic_med - perry_typic_med),
        closer_to = if_else(dist_to_ncss_rhodic < dist_to_perry_typic,
                            "NCSS Rhodic", "Perry Typic")
      )
  })
  print(three_way)

  # Save outputs
  write.csv(rhodic_rank_summary,
            "../outputs/reports_05_02/rhodic_rank_diagnostic.csv",
            row.names = FALSE)
  write.csv(feat_compare,
            "../outputs/reports_05_02/rhodic_feature_comparison.csv",
            row.names = FALSE)
  write.csv(three_way,
            "../outputs/reports_05_02/rhodic_three_way_comparison.csv",
            row.names = FALSE)

  cat("\nRhodic diagnostics saved to ../outputs/reports_05_02/\n")
}

#So Perry's Rhodics are taxonomically Rhodic but morphologically unusual compared to NCSS Rhodics — they have much more pronounced argillic/kandic features, which the model maps to non-Rhodic categories. Perry Rhodics are at the extreme end of clay translocation that NCSS Rhodics don't reach, so the model misroutes them.
#1/39 correct, but Rhodic ranked 2nd or 3rd for 25/39 pedons. Mean Rhodic probability = 0.125 when wrong. Three-way feature comparison shows Perry Rhodics are morphologically more extreme than NCSS Rhodics: clay 47% vs 38%, max_clay_ratio 11 vs 2.6, max_clay_delta 62 vs 26. The model "knows" about Rhodic but its NCSS-trained representation of Rhodic doesn't extend to Perry's extreme clay translocation.

```



```{r combined-thesis-summary}

# ============================================================
# COMBINED NCSS + PERRY SUMMARY (THESIS-READY)
# ============================================================

# NCSS internal results (from saved test metrics in bundles)
ncss_internal <- map_dfr(bundles, function(b) {
  if (is.null(b$test_metrics)) return(NULL)
  b$test_metrics %>%
    mutate(bundle = b$name, outcome = b$outcome,
           dataset = "NCSS (internal test)")
})

# Perry external results
perry_external <- perry_metric_summary %>%
  mutate(dataset = "Perry (external)") %>%
  select(bundle, outcome, dataset, accuracy, kap, bal_accuracy,
         f_meas_macro, top_k, k, n_pedons)

# Stack
combined <- bind_rows(
  ncss_internal %>% select(bundle, outcome, dataset, accuracy, kap,
                           bal_accuracy, f_meas_macro, top_k, k),
  perry_external
) %>%
  mutate(
    engine = case_when(
      str_detect(bundle, "_rf_")  ~ "rf",
      str_detect(bundle, "_xgb_") ~ "xgb"
    ),
    space = case_when(
      str_detect(bundle, "_umap_hdb$") ~ "umap_hdb",
      TRUE                              ~ "plain"
    ),
    is_min5 = str_detect(bundle, "_min5_")
  ) %>%
  select(dataset, outcome, engine, space, is_min5, bundle,
         accuracy, kap, bal_accuracy, f_meas_macro, top_k, k, n_pedons)

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("COMBINED RESULTS: NCSS internal vs Perry external\n")
cat("========================================\n")
print(combined %>%
        arrange(outcome, dataset, desc(kap)),
      n = Inf)

write.csv(combined,
          "../outputs/reports_05_02/combined_ncss_perry_results.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

# ---- Pivoted "thesis table" view ----
thesis_view <- combined %>%
  filter(!is_min5) %>%
  group_by(outcome, dataset) %>%
  slice_max(kap, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(outcome, dataset, engine, space,
         accuracy, kap, bal_accuracy, f_meas_macro, top_k) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = dataset,
    values_from = c(accuracy, kap, bal_accuracy, f_meas_macro, top_k,
                    engine, space)
  )

cat("\n--- THESIS-READY: best per outcome on each dataset ---\n")
print(thesis_view, n = Inf)

write.csv(thesis_view,
          "../outputs/reports_05_02/thesis_summary_table.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)




```

```{r, eval = FALSE}


#combine with UMAP + tuning:
# Load existing NCSS + Perry results
existing_results <- read.csv(here("outputs", "reports_05_02",
                                   "combined_ncss_perry_results.csv"))

# Build "tuned UMAP" rows from sensitivity analysis
tuned_umap_rows <- sensitivity_full %>%
  filter(status == "ok") %>%
  group_by(outcome, engine) %>%
  slice_max(ncss_kap, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # Two rows per outcome × engine — one for NCSS, one for Perry
  pivot_longer(cols = c(ncss_kap, perry_kap, ncss_acc, perry_acc),
               names_to = c("dataset_short", "metric"),
               names_sep = "_") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = metric, values_from = value) %>%
  mutate(
    dataset = if_else(dataset_short == "ncss",
                      "NCSS (internal test)", "Perry (external)"),
    space = "umap_hdb_tuned",   # distinct from plain umap_hdb
    is_min5 = FALSE,
    bundle = paste0(outcome, "_", engine, "_umap_tuned_nn",
                     n_neighbors, "_md", min_dist, "_mpts", minPts),
    bal_accuracy = NA_real_,
    f_meas_macro = NA_real_,
    top_k = NA_real_,
    k = NA_real_,
    n_pedons = NA_integer_
  ) %>%
  select(dataset, outcome, engine, space, is_min5, bundle,
         accuracy = acc, kap, bal_accuracy, f_meas_macro,
         top_k, k, n_pedons)

# Combine
combined_with_tuned <- bind_rows(existing_results, tuned_umap_rows)

write.csv(combined_with_tuned,
          here("outputs", "reports_05_02",
                "combined_ncss_perry_with_tuned_umap.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)
```


```{r done}

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("DONE.\n")
cat("Outputs written to ../outputs/reports_05_02/:\n")
cat("- perry_bundle_inventory.csv\n")
cat("- domain_shift_result.rds\n")
cat("- perry_predictions_long.csv\n")
cat("- perry_external_validation.csv\n")
cat("- perry_hierarchical_accuracy.csv\n")
cat("- combined_ncss_perry_results.csv\n")
cat("- thesis_summary_table.csv\n")
cat("========================================\n")
beep("complete")
```

```{r figures!}
# ============================================================
# THESIS FIGURES
# ============================================================
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(scales)

fig_dir <- "../outputs/figures_05_02"
dir.create(fig_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Common theme
thesis_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "top",
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA)
  )

# Order taxonomic levels from coarse to fine
tax_order <- c("taxorder", "taxsuborder", "taxgrtgroup",
               "taxsubgrp_mod1", "taxpartsize", "taxonname_clean")
tax_labels <- c("Order", "Suborder", "Great Group",
                "Subgroup", "Particle Size", "Series")
names(tax_labels) <- tax_order

# Pull n_classes from bundle inventory
n_classes_lookup <- bundle_inventory %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  summarise(n_classes_max = max(n_classes, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

fig1_data <- combined %>%
  filter(!is_min5) %>%
  group_by(outcome, dataset) %>%
  slice_max(kap, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  left_join(n_classes_lookup, by = "outcome") %>%
  mutate(
    outcome = factor(outcome, levels = tax_order, labels = tax_labels[tax_order]),
    dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("NCSS (internal test)", "Perry (external)"))
  )

# n_classes label - one per outcome (top of bar group)
n_class_labels <- fig1_data %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  summarise(n_classes_max = first(n_classes_max),
            ymax = max(kap, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

fig1 <- ggplot(fig1_data, aes(x = outcome, y = kap, fill = dataset)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(kap, 2)),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
            vjust = -0.4, size = 3.2) +
  geom_text(data = n_class_labels,
            aes(x = outcome, y = 1.02, label = paste0("k=", n_classes_max)),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 3.4, fontface = "italic", color = "grey30") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("NCSS (internal test)" = "#2C7BB6",
                                "Perry (external)" = "#D7191C"),
                    name = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.08), expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Cohen's Kappa",
       title = "Model performance degrades from coarse to fine taxonomic levels",
       subtitle = "Best-performing variant per outcome (k = number of classes)") +
  thesis_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

print(fig1)
ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig1_kappa_by_level.png"),
       fig1, width = 9, height = 5.5, dpi = 300)
library(here)
 
fig_dir <- here("outputs", "figures_05_02")
 
thesis_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "top",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA))
 
tax_labels <- c(
  taxorder        = "Order",
  taxsuborder     = "Suborder",
  taxgrtgroup     = "Great Group",
  taxsubgrp_mod1  = "Subgroup",
  taxpartsize     = "Particle Size",
  taxonname_clean = "Series"
)
 
# Define the pair-label order from coarsest (suborder → order) to finest (series → subgroup)
pair_order <- c(
  "Order → Suborder",
  "Suborder → Great Group",
  "Great Group → Subgroup",
  "Subgroup → Series"
)
 
fig2_data <- hier_acc %>%
  mutate(
    # Flip the arrow direction: Parent → Child (reads as going down the hierarchy)
    pair_label = paste0(tax_labels[parent], " → ", tax_labels[child]),
    pair_label = factor(pair_label, levels = c(
      "Order → Suborder",
      "Suborder → Great Group",
      "Great Group → Subgroup",
      "Subgroup → Series"
    ))
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(child_acc, parent_acc, parent_acc_when_child_wrong),
               names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    metric = recode(metric,
                    child_acc = "Child correct",
                    parent_acc = "Parent correct",
                    parent_acc_when_child_wrong = "Parent correct when child wrong"),
    metric = factor(metric, levels = c("Parent correct when child wrong",
                                        "Parent correct",
                                        "Child correct"))
  )
 
fig2 <- ggplot(fig2_data, aes(x = pair_label, y = value, fill = metric)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(is.na(value), "", round(value, 2))),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
            vjust = -0.4, size = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Parent correct when child wrong" = "#FDAE61",
               "Parent correct"                  = "#1A9641",
               "Child correct"                   = "#2C7BB6"),
    breaks = c("Parent correct when child wrong",
               "Parent correct",
               "Child correct"),
    name = NULL
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.05), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Accuracy",
       title = "Graceful degradation: parent class is recovered when child fails",
       subtitle = "When the model misses the fine class, the level above is usually right") +
  thesis_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 15, hjust = 1))
 
print(fig2)
ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig2_hierarchical_accuracy.png"),
       fig2, width = 10, height = 5.5, dpi = 300)
 
library(cowplot)
library(patchwork)
# ============================================================
# BUILD CONFUSION MATRIX PLOTS FOR EACH OUTCOME
# Uses the best Perry-side bundle per outcome.
# ============================================================
cm_plots <- list()

for (i in seq_len(nrow(best_perry_bundle))) {
  bn <- best_perry_bundle$bundle[i]
  oc <- best_perry_bundle$outcome[i]
  
  v <- perry_validations[[bn]]
  if (is.null(v) || is.null(v$data)) next
  
  # Get the joined data with predictions and truth
  joined <- v$data %>%
    mutate(
      truth_fct = as.character(truth_fct),
      pred_fct = as.character(pred_fct)
    )
  
  # Build confusion matrix counts
  cm_data <- joined %>%
    count(truth_fct, pred_fct, name = "n") %>%
    group_by(truth_fct) %>%
    mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Get class order — sort by truth frequency (most common at top of y-axis)
  class_order <- joined %>%
    count(truth_fct, name = "n") %>%
    arrange(n) %>%
    pull(truth_fct)
  
  # Use union of truth and prediction classes for x-axis
  all_classes <- union(unique(joined$truth_fct), unique(joined$pred_fct))
  x_order <- intersect(class_order, all_classes)
  if (length(setdiff(all_classes, x_order)) > 0) {
    x_order <- c(x_order, setdiff(all_classes, x_order))
  }
  
  cm_data <- cm_data %>%
    mutate(
      truth_fct = factor(truth_fct, levels = class_order),
      pred_fct = factor(pred_fct, levels = x_order)
    )
  
  # Build the plot
  cm_plots[[oc]] <- ggplot(cm_data, aes(x = pred_fct, y = truth_fct, fill = prop)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
    geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(n > 0, n, "")), size = 6) +
    scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#2C7BB6",
                        limits = c(0, 1),
                        name = "Row prop") +
    labs(title = tax_labels[oc],
         x = "Predicted class",
         y = "True class") +
    thesis_theme +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "right"
    )
}

cat("Built confusion matrix plots for outcomes:",
    paste(names(cm_plots), collapse = ", "), "\n")
# Reorder by tax_order
ordered_cm_plots <- cm_plots[tax_order]
ordered_cm_plots <- ordered_cm_plots[!sapply(ordered_cm_plots, is.null)]

# Extract the legend from the first plot
shared_legend_cm <- get_legend(ordered_cm_plots[[1]])

# Remove the legend from every panel
ordered_cm_plots_no_legend <- map(ordered_cm_plots, function(p) {
  p + scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "white", high = "#2C7BB6",
    limits = c(0, 1),
    guide = "none"
  )
})

# Build patchwork of panels (no legend on any)
cm_panels <- wrap_plots(ordered_cm_plots_no_legend, ncol = 2) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Perry confusion matrices across taxonomic levels",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))
  )

# Combine panels + legend in a horizontal layout
fig3 <- plot_grid(cm_panels, shared_legend_cm,
                  ncol = 2,
                  rel_widths = c(1, 0.08))

print(fig3)
ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig3_confusion_matrices.png"),
       fig3, width = 16, height = 15, dpi = 300)
# Use the three_way table from your existing rhodic chunk
fig4_data <- three_way %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(ncss_rhodic_med, perry_rhodic_med, perry_typic_med),
               names_to = "group", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    group = recode(group,
                   ncss_rhodic_med = "NCSS Rhodic (training)",
                   perry_rhodic_med = "Perry Rhodic (truth)",
                   perry_typic_med = "Perry Typic"),
    group = factor(group, levels = c("NCSS Rhodic (training)",
                                      "Perry Rhodic (truth)",
                                      "Perry Typic"))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(value))

# Pretty feature labels
feat_labels <- c(
  R_w = "Red (RGB)", G_w = "Green (RGB)", B_w = "Blue (RGB)",
  clay_w = "Clay %", sand_w = "Sand %", silt_w = "Silt %",
  depth_to_Bt_cm = "Depth to Bt (cm)",
  bt_thickness_cm = "Bt thickness (cm)",
  max_clay_ratio_0_150 = "Max clay ratio (0-150 cm)",
  max_clay_delta_0_150 = "Max clay delta (0-150 cm)"
)

fig4_data <- fig4_data %>%
  mutate(feature = factor(feature, levels = names(feat_labels),
                          labels = feat_labels[names(feat_labels)]))

fig4 <- ggplot(fig4_data, aes(x = group, y = value, fill = group)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~ feature, scales = "free_y", ncol = 4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("NCSS Rhodic (training)" = "#D7191C",
                                "Perry Rhodic (truth)" = "#FDAE61",
                                "Perry Typic" = "#ABD9E9"),
                    name = NULL) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Median value",
       title = "Perry Rhodics are morphologically more extreme than NCSS Rhodics",
       subtitle = "Higher clay translocation, thinner Bt — out of NCSS training distribution") +
  thesis_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 10))

print(fig4)
ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig4_rhodic_features.png"),
       fig4, width = 12, height = 7, dpi = 300)

# Two-panel: accuracy on top, bal_accuracy on bottom
fig5_data <- combined %>%
  filter(!is_min5) %>%
  group_by(outcome, dataset) %>%
  slice_max(kap, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  left_join(n_classes_lookup, by = "outcome") %>%
  mutate(
    outcome = factor(outcome, levels = tax_order, labels = tax_labels[tax_order]),
    dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("NCSS (internal test)", "Perry (external)"))
  )

n_class_labels5 <- fig5_data %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  summarise(n_classes_max = first(n_classes_max), .groups = "drop")

# Long format for two facets
fig5_long <- fig5_data %>%
  select(outcome, dataset, accuracy, bal_accuracy) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(accuracy, bal_accuracy),
               names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(metric = recode(metric,
                         accuracy = "Accuracy",
                         bal_accuracy = "Balanced Accuracy"),
         metric = factor(metric, levels = c("Accuracy", "Balanced Accuracy")))

fig5 <- ggplot(fig5_long, aes(x = outcome, y = value, fill = dataset)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(is.na(value), "", round(value, 2))),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
            vjust = -0.4, size = 3) +
  geom_text(data = n_class_labels5 %>%
              tidyr::crossing(metric = factor(c("Accuracy", "Balanced Accuracy"),
                                              levels = c("Accuracy", "Balanced Accuracy"))),
            aes(x = outcome, y = 1.02, label = paste0("k=", n_classes_max)),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 3.2, fontface = "italic", color = "grey30") +
  facet_wrap(~ metric, ncol = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("NCSS (internal test)" = "#2C7BB6",
                                "Perry (external)" = "#D7191C"),
                    name = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.08), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL,
       title = "Accuracy vs. balanced accuracy across taxonomic levels",
       subtitle = "Plain accuracy hides class-imbalance failures (k = n classes)") +
  thesis_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))

print(fig5)
ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig5_accuracy_balaccuracy.png"),
       fig5, width = 9, height = 8, dpi = 300)



library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(ggplot2)

fig_dir <- here("outputs", "figures_05_02")
dir.create(fig_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

thesis_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "top",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA))

tax_order  <- c("taxorder", "taxsuborder", "taxgrtgroup",
                "taxsubgrp_mod1", "taxpartsize", "taxonname_clean")
tax_labels <- c("Order", "Suborder", "Great Group",
                "Subgroup", "Particle Size", "Series")
names(tax_labels) <- tax_order

# Load NCSS bootstrapped summary (10 reps)
ncss_summary <- read.csv(here("outputs", "reports_05_02",
                               "test_metrics_summary_clean.csv")) %>%
  mutate(
    dataset = "NCSS (internal test)",
    kap_value = kap_median,
    kap_lo = kap_lo_95,
    kap_hi = kap_hi_95
  ) %>%
  select(outcome, engine, space, dataset, kap_value, kap_lo, kap_hi)

# Load Perry results (point estimates from canonical bundles)
perry_results <- read.csv(here("outputs", "reports_05_02",
                                "perry_external_validation.csv")) %>%
  filter(!is_min5 | outcome != "taxonname_clean") %>%   # use min_n=15 series for parity with NCSS bootstrap
  mutate(
    dataset = "Perry (external)",
    kap_value = kap,
    kap_lo = NA_real_,
    kap_hi = NA_real_
  ) %>%
  select(outcome, engine, space, dataset, kap_value, kap_lo, kap_hi)

# Combine
fig_data <- bind_rows(ncss_summary, perry_results) %>%
  mutate(
    outcome = factor(outcome, levels = tax_order, labels = tax_labels[tax_order]),
    dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("NCSS (internal test)", "Perry (external)")),
    variant = paste(engine, space, sep = " / "),
    variant = recode(variant,
                     "rf / plain"     = "RF (plain)",
                     "xgb / plain"    = "XGB (plain)",
                     "rf / umap_hdb"  = "RF (UMAP+HDB)",
                     "xgb / umap_hdb" = "XGB (UMAP+HDB)"),
    variant = factor(variant, levels = c("RF (plain)", "XGB (plain)",
                                          "RF (UMAP+HDB)", "XGB (UMAP+HDB)"))
  )

# Build the figure: dodge by variant within each outcome, color by dataset
fig_perry_variants <- ggplot(fig_data,
                              aes(x = outcome, y = kap_value,
                                  color = variant, shape = dataset)) +
  # Error bars only show for NCSS (Perry has NA bounds)
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = kap_lo, ymax = kap_hi),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
                width = 0.25, na.rm = TRUE, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_point(size = 3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.7)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("RF (plain)"     = "#2C7BB6",
                                 "XGB (plain)"    = "#1A9641",
                                 "RF (UMAP+HDB)"  = "#FDAE61",
                                 "XGB (UMAP+HDB)" = "#D7191C"),
                     name = "Model variant") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("NCSS (internal test)" = 16,
                                 "Perry (external)" = 17),
                     name = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.05, 1.05), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Cohen's Kappa",
       title = "Model variant comparison: NCSS internal vs. Perry external",
       subtitle = "NCSS error bars: 95% range across 10 train/test replicates;\n Perry: canonical-bundle point estimate") +
  thesis_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "right",
        legend.box = "vertical")

print(fig_perry_variants)
ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig_perry_variants_comparison.png"),
       fig_perry_variants, width = 11, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```
```{r}
#perry performance:

fig_dir <- here("outputs", "figures_05_02")

thesis_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "top",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA))

tax_order  <- c("taxorder", "taxsuborder", "taxgrtgroup",
                "taxsubgrp_mod1", "taxpartsize", "taxonname_clean")
tax_labels <- c("Order", "Suborder", "Great Group",
                "Subgroup", "Particle Size", "Series")
names(tax_labels) <- tax_order

# Load Perry results
perry_results <- read.csv(here("outputs", "reports_05_02",
                                "perry_external_validation.csv")) %>%
  filter(is_min5 | outcome != "taxonname_clean")   # min_n=5 for series

perry_for_plot <- perry_results %>%
  mutate(
    outcome = factor(outcome, levels = tax_order, labels = tax_labels[tax_order]),
    variant = paste(engine, space, sep = " / "),
    variant = recode(variant,
                     "rf / plain"     = "RF (plain)",
                     "xgb / plain"    = "XGB (plain)",
                     "rf / umap_hdb"  = "RF (UMAP+HDB)",
                     "xgb / umap_hdb" = "XGB (UMAP+HDB)"),
    variant = factor(variant, levels = c("RF (plain)", "XGB (plain)",
                                          "RF (UMAP+HDB)", "XGB (UMAP+HDB)"))
  )

fig_perry_dots <- ggplot(perry_for_plot,
                         aes(x = outcome, y = kap,
                             color = variant, fill = variant)) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
             size = 4, shape = 21, color = "black", stroke = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("RF (plain)"     = "#2C7BB6",
                                "XGB (plain)"    = "#1A9641",
                                "RF (UMAP+HDB)"  = "#FDAE61",
                                "XGB (UMAP+HDB)" = "#D7191C"),
                    name = NULL) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("RF (plain)"     = "#2C7BB6",
                                 "XGB (plain)"    = "#1A9641",
                                 "RF (UMAP+HDB)"  = "#FDAE61",
                                 "XGB (UMAP+HDB)" = "#D7191C"),
                     guide = "none") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.05, 1.05), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Cohen's Kappa",
       title = "Perry external performance by model variant at each taxonomic level",
       subtitle = "Plain features dominate at fine taxonomic levels; UMAP+HDB only wins at order") +
  thesis_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "top")

print(fig_perry_dots)
ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig_perry_variability_dots.png"),
       fig_perry_dots, width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

```{r predictor table}
# ============================================================
# PREDICTOR INVENTORY TABLE - export to CSV
# ============================================================

predictor_table <- tibble::tribble(
  ~Category, ~Predictor, ~Description,

  # Location
  "Location", "POINT_X", "Easting/longitude coordinate",
  "Location", "POINT_Y", "Northing/latitude coordinate",

  # Texture
  "Texture (0-150 cm, depth-weighted)", "sand_w", "Sand fraction (%)",
  "Texture (0-150 cm, depth-weighted)", "silt_w", "Silt fraction (%)",
  "Texture (0-150 cm, depth-weighted)", "clay_w", "Clay fraction (%)",

  # Color
  "Color (Munsell to sRGB, depth-weighted)", "R_w", "Red channel (0-1)",
  "Color (Munsell to sRGB, depth-weighted)", "G_w", "Green channel (0-1)",
  "Color (Munsell to sRGB, depth-weighted)", "B_w", "Blue channel (0-1)",

  # Chemistry (NCSS only)
  "Chemistry (NCSS only; imputed in Perry)", "cec7_w", "Cation exchange capacity at pH 7 (cmol/kg, weighted)",
  "Chemistry (NCSS only; imputed in Perry)", "pct_hz_with_cec7", "Proportion of horizons with measured CEC7",
  "Chemistry (NCSS only; imputed in Perry)", "min_cec_per_kg_clay_0_150", "Minimum CEC per kg clay in 0-150 cm zone",
  "Chemistry (NCSS only; imputed in Perry)", "median_cec_per_kg_clay_bt_zone", "Median CEC per kg clay within Bt zone",

  # Horizon suffix flags
  "Horizon suffix flags", "any_t_suffix", "Any horizon with 't' (illuvial clay)",
  "Horizon suffix flags", "any_g_suffix", "Any horizon with 'g' (gleyed)",
  "Horizon suffix flags", "any_x_suffix", "Any horizon with 'x' (fragipan)",
  "Horizon suffix flags", "any_k_suffix", "Any horizon with 'k' (carbonate accumulation)",

  # Horizon designation proportions
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_A", "Proportion of profile in A horizon",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Ap", "Proportion in Ap horizon (plowed)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_AB", "Proportion in AB transition",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_AE", "Proportion in AE transition",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_E", "Proportion in E horizon",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_EA", "Proportion in EA transition",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_EB", "Proportion in EB transition",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_B", "Proportion in B horizon",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_BA", "Proportion in BA transition",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_BC", "Proportion in BC transition",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_BE", "Proportion in BE transition",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Bw", "Proportion in Bw horizon",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Bt", "Proportion in Bt horizon (illuvial clay)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Btv", "Proportion in Btv (with plinthite)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Btx", "Proportion in Btx (with fragipan)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Btc", "Proportion in Btc (with concretions)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Btg", "Proportion in Btg (gleyed Bt)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Btgv", "Proportion in Btgv",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Btgx", "Proportion in Btgx",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Btvc", "Proportion in Btvc",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Btvx", "Proportion in Btvx",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Btcv", "Proportion in Btcv",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Btcn", "Proportion in Btcn",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Btcb", "Proportion in Btcb (buried)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Btb", "Proportion in Btb (buried Bt)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Btgb", "Proportion in Btgb (buried gleyed)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Btss", "Proportion in Btss (slickensides)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Bg", "Proportion in Bg horizon",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Bx", "Proportion in Bx horizon",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Bh", "Proportion in Bh (illuvial humus)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Bc", "Proportion in Bc horizon",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Bwc", "Proportion in Bwc horizon",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Bwb", "Proportion in Bwb (buried)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Eg", "Proportion in Eg (gleyed E)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Ex", "Proportion in Ex",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Eb", "Proportion in Eb (buried)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Ec", "Proportion in Ec",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Ag", "Proportion in Ag (gleyed A)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Ab", "Proportion in Ab (buried A)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Ac", "Proportion in Ac",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Acb", "Proportion in Acb",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Apc", "Proportion in Apc",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_C", "Proportion in C horizon",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Cc", "Proportion in Cc",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Cg", "Proportion in Cg (gleyed C)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Cr", "Proportion in Cr (weathered bedrock)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_R", "Proportion in R (bedrock)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Oa", "Proportion in Oa (sapric organic)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Oe", "Proportion in Oe (hemic organic)",
  "Horizon designation proportions", "prop_hzbase_Unknown", "Proportion of unparseable horizons",

  # Bt horizon depth/thickness
  "Bt horizon depth and thickness", "depth_to_Bt_cm", "Depth to top of any Bt (cm)",
  "Bt horizon depth and thickness", "bt_top_cm", "Depth to top of Bt-like horizon (cm)",
  "Bt horizon depth and thickness", "bt_thickness_cm", "Total Bt thickness (cm)",
  "Bt horizon depth and thickness", "bt_thk_0_150_cm", "Bt thickness within 0-150 cm zone",
  "Bt horizon depth and thickness", "has_Bt_0_150", "Bt present within 0-150 cm (binary)",
  "Bt horizon depth and thickness", "has_bt_like", "Any Bt-like horizon present (binary)",
  "Bt horizon depth and thickness", "has_bt_below_mantle", "Bt occurs below sandy mantle (binary)",

  # Argillic horizon
  "Argillic horizon", "depth_to_argillic_cm", "Depth to top of argillic horizon (cm)",
  "Argillic horizon", "argillic_top_cm", "Argillic top depth (cm)",
  "Argillic horizon", "argillic_thickness_cm", "Argillic thickness (cm)",
  "Argillic horizon", "argillic_thk_0_150_cm", "Argillic thickness within 0-150 cm",
  "Argillic horizon", "argillic_contiguous_max_cm", "Maximum contiguous argillic thickness (cm)",
  "Argillic horizon", "has_argillic_0_150", "Argillic present in 0-150 cm (binary)",

  # E horizon
  "E horizon", "depth_to_E_cm", "Depth to top of E horizon (cm)",
  "E horizon", "has_E_0_150", "E horizon present in 0-150 cm (binary)",

  # Gley features
  "Gley features", "depth_to_gley_cm", "Depth to first gleyed horizon (cm)",
  "Gley features", "has_gley_0_150", "Gley present in 0-150 cm (binary)",

  # Sandy mantle / surface texture
  "Sandy mantle and surface texture", "sandy_mantle_cm", "Thickness of surface sandy mantle (cm)",
  "Sandy mantle and surface texture", "depth_to_first_nonsandy_cm", "Depth to first non-sandy horizon (cm)",
  "Sandy mantle and surface texture", "sandy_layers_n_surface", "Number of contiguous sandy surface layers",
  "Sandy mantle and surface texture", "pct_sandy_0_150", "Percent of 0-150 cm with sandy texture",
  "Sandy mantle and surface texture", "mean_sand_0_150", "Mean sand content 0-150 cm (%)",
  "Sandy mantle and surface texture", "mean_clay_0_150", "Mean clay content 0-150 cm (%)",
  "Sandy mantle and surface texture", "delta_clay_at_mantle_break", "Clay change at sandy/Bt boundary (%)",
  "Sandy mantle and surface texture", "delta_sand_at_mantle_break", "Sand change at sandy/Bt boundary (%)",
  "Sandy mantle and surface texture", "abrupt_sand_to_bt_transition", "Abrupt sand-to-Bt transition (binary)",

  # Clay translocation indices
  "Clay translocation indices", "max_clay_ratio_0_150", "Maximum clay ratio in 0-150 cm",
  "Clay translocation indices", "max_clay_delta_0_150", "Maximum absolute clay increase (%)",
  "Clay translocation indices", "depth_to_first_clay_jump", "Depth where clay first jumps (cm)",
  "Clay translocation indices", "has_major_clay_drop_within_150", "Major clay decrease within 150 cm (binary)",
  "Clay translocation indices", "depth_to_20pct_clay_decrease", "Depth to 20% clay drop (cm)",
  "Clay translocation indices", "clay_persistence_index_0_150", "Clay persistence index in 0-150 cm",

  # Plinthite features
  "Plinthite features", "plinthite_flag", "Any plinthite present (binary)",
  "Plinthite features", "plinthite_within_150cm_flag", "Plinthite within 150 cm (binary)",
  "Plinthite features", "top_depth_plinthite", "Depth to top of plinthite (cm)",
  "Plinthite features", "plinthite_thickness_cm", "Total plinthite thickness (cm)",
  "Plinthite features", "pct_plinthite_by_hz", "Percent of horizons containing plinthite",
  "Plinthite features", "pct_plinthite_by_thickness", "Percent of profile thickness with plinthite",
  "Plinthite features", "plinthite_overlap_0_150_cm", "Plinthite overlap with 0-150 cm zone"
)

cat("Total predictors in table:", nrow(predictor_table), "\n")

write.csv(predictor_table,
          "../outputs/reports_05_02/predictor_inventory.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

cat("Saved to: ../outputs/reports_05_02/predictor_inventory.csv\n")
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)

fig_dir <- here("outputs", "figures_05_02")
dir.create(fig_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

thesis_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "top",
        legend.box = "horizontal",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA))

tax_order  <- c("taxorder", "taxsuborder", "taxgrtgroup",
                "taxsubgrp_mod1", "taxpartsize", "taxonname_clean")
tax_labels <- c("Order", "Suborder", "Great Group",
                "Subgroup", "Particle Size", "Series")
names(tax_labels) <- tax_order

# ============================================================
# REBUILD NCSS SUMMARY FROM ALL-REPS FILE
# ============================================================
reps <- read.csv(here("outputs", "reports_05_02",
                       "test_metrics_all_reps_ncss.csv"))

reps <- reps %>%
  mutate(is_min5 = str_detect(bundle_name, "_min5_"))

ncss_summary <- reps %>%
  group_by(outcome, engine, space, is_min5) %>%
  summarise(
    n_reps = n(),
    kap_median = median(kap, na.rm = TRUE),
    kap_lo_95 = quantile(kap, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    kap_hi_95 = quantile(kap, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# NCSS: use min_n=15 for series (the bootstrapped version)
# All other outcomes: use the standard bootstrap (no is_min5 distinction)
ncss_for_plot <- ncss_summary %>%
  filter(is_min5 == FALSE) %>%  # only the non-min5 bootstrapped bundles
  mutate(dataset = "NCSS (internal test)") %>%
  select(outcome, engine, space, is_min5, dataset,
         kap_median, kap_lo_95, kap_hi_95)

cat("NCSS data ready (uses min_n=15 series):\n")
print(ncss_for_plot %>% arrange(outcome, desc(kap_median)))

# ============================================================
# PERRY: use min_n=5 for series, drop min_n=15
# ============================================================
perry_for_plot <- read.csv(here("outputs", "reports_05_02",
                                 "perry_external_validation.csv")) %>%
  filter(!(outcome == "taxonname_clean" & is_min5 == FALSE)) %>%
  mutate(
    dataset = "Perry (external)",
    kap_median = kap,
    kap_lo_95 = NA_real_,
    kap_hi_95 = NA_real_
  ) %>%
  select(outcome, engine, space, is_min5, dataset,
         kap_median, kap_lo_95, kap_hi_95)

cat("\nPerry data ready (uses min_n=5 series):\n")
print(perry_for_plot %>% arrange(outcome, desc(kap_median)))

# ============================================================
# n_classes_lookup
# ============================================================
n_classes_lookup <- read.csv(here("outputs", "reports_05_02",
                                   "perry_bundle_inventory.csv")) %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  summarise(n_classes_max = max(n_classes, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# ============================================================
# COMBINE AND PICK BEST VARIANT PER (outcome × dataset)
# ============================================================
fig1_data <- bind_rows(ncss_for_plot, perry_for_plot) %>%
  group_by(outcome, dataset) %>%
  slice_max(kap_median, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  left_join(n_classes_lookup, by = "outcome") %>%
  mutate(
    outcome_lbl = factor(outcome, levels = tax_order, labels = tax_labels[tax_order]),
    dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("NCSS (internal test)", "Perry (external)")),
    variant = paste(
      toupper(engine),
      ifelse(space == "umap_hdb", "(UMAP+HDB)", "(plain)")
    ),
    variant = factor(variant, levels = c("RF (plain)", "XGB (plain)",
                                          "RF (UMAP+HDB)", "XGB (UMAP+HDB)"))
  )

cat("\nBest variant per (outcome × dataset):\n")
print(fig1_data %>% select(outcome, dataset, variant, kap_median, kap_lo_95, kap_hi_95))

n_class_labels <- fig1_data %>%
  group_by(outcome_lbl) %>%
  summarise(n_classes_max = first(n_classes_max), .groups = "drop")

# ============================================================
# BUILD THE FIGURE
# Color = dataset (NCSS vs Perry)
# Shape on points = variant (RF/XGB × plain/UMAP+HDB)
# ============================================================
fig1 <- ggplot(fig1_data, aes(x = outcome_lbl, y = kap_median)) +
  # Bars colored by dataset
  geom_col(aes(fill = dataset),
           position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
           width = 0.7, alpha = 0.85) +
  # Error bars (NCSS only — Perry has NA)
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = kap_lo_95, ymax = kap_hi_95, group = dataset),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
                width = 0.25, na.rm = TRUE, linewidth = 0.5) +
  # Variant marker on top of each bar
  geom_point(aes(shape = variant, group = dataset),
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
             size = 3, color = "black", fill = "white", stroke = 1) +
  # Kappa value above each bar
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", kap_median), group = dataset),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
            vjust = -1.4, size = 3.2) +
  # n_classes annotation at top
  geom_text(data = n_class_labels,
            aes(x = outcome_lbl, y = 1.10, label = paste0("k=", n_classes_max)),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 3.4, fontface = "italic", color = "grey30") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("NCSS (internal test)" = "#2C7BB6",
                                "Perry (external)" = "#D7191C"),
                    name = "Dataset") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("RF (plain)" = 21,
                                 "XGB (plain)" = 22,
                                 "RF (UMAP+HDB)" = 24,
                                 "XGB (UMAP+HDB)" = 23),
                     name = "Best variant",
                     drop = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.05, 1.15), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Cohen's Kappa (median ± 95% range, NCSS only)",
       title = "Model performance degrades from coarse to fine taxonomic levels",
       subtitle = paste(
         "Best variant per (outcome × dataset). NCSS bars: median ± 95% range across 10 replicates.",
         "Perry bars: canonical-bundle point estimate. Series: NCSS min_n=15, Perry min_n=5.",
         sep = "\n"
       )) +
  thesis_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "top",
        legend.box = "horizontal")

print(fig1)
ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig1_kappa_by_level_with_ci.png"),
       fig1, width = 11, height = 6.5, dpi = 300)

cat("\nFigure saved.\n")
```

```{r}
library(patchwork)
fig_dir <- here("outputs", "figures_05_02")
dir.create(fig_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

thesis_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "top",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA))

tax_order  <- c("taxorder", "taxsuborder", "taxgrtgroup",
                "taxsubgrp_mod1", "taxpartsize", "taxonname_clean")
tax_labels <- c("Order", "Suborder", "Great Group",
                "Subgroup", "Particle Size", "Series")
names(tax_labels) <- tax_order

# ============================================================
# IDENTIFY BEST VARIANT PER OUTCOME (by Perry kap)
# ============================================================
# Series gets min_n=5 (the deployment-realistic version)
best_perry_per_outcome <- read.csv(
  here("outputs", "reports_05_02", "perry_external_validation.csv")
) %>%
  filter(is_min5 | outcome != "taxonname_clean") %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  slice_max(kap, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

cat("Best Perry variant per outcome:\n")
print(best_perry_per_outcome %>% select(outcome, engine, space, is_min5, bundle, kap))

# ============================================================
# PULL PERRY PREDICTIONS WITH PROBABILITIES
# perry_preds is a list of tibbles, indexed by bundle name —
# already produced in your deploy chunk and lives in memory.
# ============================================================
compute_perry_rank <- function(pred_df, truth_df, bundle_name, outcome) {
  pred_df <- pred_df %>% mutate(peiid = as.character(peiid))
  truth_df <- truth_df %>% mutate(peiid = as.character(peiid))
  
  # Join with truth, keep only pedons with unambiguous truth
  joined <- pred_df %>%
    left_join(truth_df %>% select(peiid, truth, n_truth_unique), by = "peiid") %>%
    filter(!is.na(truth), n_truth_unique == 1)
  
  if (nrow(joined) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  prob_cols <- names(joined)[
    str_detect(names(joined), "^\\.pred_") & names(joined) != ".pred_class"
  ]
  class_names <- str_remove(prob_cols, "^\\.pred_")
  
  map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(joined)), function(i) {
    p <- as.numeric(joined[i, prob_cols])
    names(p) <- class_names
    sorted <- sort(p, decreasing = TRUE)
    
    truth <- joined$truth[i]
    rank_truth <- which(names(sorted) == truth)
    if (length(rank_truth) == 0) rank_truth <- NA_integer_
    
    tibble(
      peiid = joined$peiid[i],
      outcome = outcome,
      bundle_name = bundle_name,
      truth = truth,
      rank1_class = names(sorted)[1],
      rank2_class = if (length(sorted) >= 2) names(sorted)[2] else NA_character_,
      rank3_class = if (length(sorted) >= 3) names(sorted)[3] else NA_character_,
      rank_of_truth = rank_truth,
      pred_top1 = names(sorted)[1]
    )
  })
}

# Apply across the chosen best-variant bundles
perry_ranks_full <- map_dfr(seq_len(nrow(best_perry_per_outcome)), function(i) {
  bn <- best_perry_per_outcome$bundle[i]
  oc <- best_perry_per_outcome$outcome[i]
  truth_key <- oc  # all outcomes use their own name as truth key
  
  pred_df <- perry_preds[[bn]]
  truth_df <- perry_truth[[truth_key]]
  
  if (is.null(pred_df) || is.null(truth_df)) {
    cat("Missing data for", bn, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  compute_perry_rank(pred_df, truth_df, bn, oc)
})

cat("\nTotal Perry ranked predictions:", nrow(perry_ranks_full), "\n")
write.csv(perry_ranks_full,
          here("outputs", "reports_05_02", "perry_test_ranks.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

# ============================================================
# PLOT
# ============================================================
plot_perry_rank <- function(rank_df, outcome_label) {
  rank_df <- rank_df %>%
    filter(!is.na(rank_of_truth)) %>%
    mutate(
      rank_category = case_when(
        rank_of_truth == 1 ~ "Rank 1\n(correct)",
        rank_of_truth == 2 ~ "Rank 2\n(close miss)",
        rank_of_truth == 3 ~ "Rank 3",
        rank_of_truth >= 4 & rank_of_truth <= 5 ~ "Rank 4-5",
        rank_of_truth >= 6 ~ "Rank 6+\n(off)"
      ),
      rank_category = factor(rank_category,
                             levels = c("Rank 1\n(correct)",
                                        "Rank 2\n(close miss)",
                                        "Rank 3",
                                        "Rank 4-5",
                                        "Rank 6+\n(off)"))
    )
  
  # Order classes by total count (most common at top)
  truth_order <- rank_df %>% count(truth) %>% arrange(n) %>% pull(truth)
  
  # Count and proportion per (truth × rank_category)
  rank_summary <- rank_df %>%
    count(truth, rank_category) %>%
    group_by(truth) %>%
    mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(truth = factor(truth, levels = truth_order))
  
  # "What was predicted instead" for non-rank-1 cells
  predicted_instead <- rank_df %>%
    filter(rank_of_truth >= 2) %>%
    mutate(
      rank_category = case_when(
        rank_of_truth == 2 ~ "Rank 2\n(close miss)",
        rank_of_truth == 3 ~ "Rank 3",
        rank_of_truth >= 4 & rank_of_truth <= 5 ~ "Rank 4-5",
        rank_of_truth >= 6 ~ "Rank 6+\n(off)"
      ),
      rank_category = factor(rank_category,
                             levels = c("Rank 1\n(correct)",
                                        "Rank 2\n(close miss)",
                                        "Rank 3",
                                        "Rank 4-5",
                                        "Rank 6+\n(off)"))
    ) %>%
    count(truth, rank_category, pred_top1, sort = TRUE) %>%
    group_by(truth, rank_category) %>%
    slice_max(n, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    rename(top_pred_label = pred_top1)
  
  rank_summary <- rank_summary %>%
    left_join(predicted_instead %>% select(truth, rank_category, top_pred_label),
              by = c("truth", "rank_category"))
  
  rank_summary <- rank_summary %>%
    mutate(
      cell_label = case_when(
        n == 0 ~ "",
        rank_category == "Rank 1\n(correct)" ~ as.character(n),
        !is.na(top_pred_label) ~ paste0(n, "\n→ ", top_pred_label),
        TRUE ~ as.character(n)
      )
    )
  
  ggplot(rank_summary, aes(x = rank_category, y = truth, fill = prop)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white") +
    geom_text(aes(label = cell_label), size = 4, lineheight = 0.85) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#D55E00", mid = "#E69F00", high = "#009E73",
                         midpoint = 0.3,
                         limits = c(0, 1),
                         name = "Row prop") +
    labs(x = "Rank of truth in model's predictions",
         y = "True class",
         title = outcome_label) +
    thesis_theme +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5, size = 8),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
          panel.grid = element_blank(),
          legend.position = "right")
}

# Build per-outcome plots
perry_rank_plots <- list()
outcomes_with_data <- perry_ranks_full %>% distinct(outcome) %>% pull(outcome)

for (oc in outcomes_with_data) {
  rd <- perry_ranks_full %>% filter(outcome == oc)
  if (nrow(rd) == 0) next
  perry_rank_plots[[oc]] <- plot_perry_rank(rd, tax_labels[oc])
}

# Save individual plots
for (oc in names(perry_rank_plots)) {
  print(perry_rank_plots[[oc]])
  ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, paste0("fig_perry_rank_", oc, ".png")),
         perry_rank_plots[[oc]],
         width = 11, height = 8, dpi = 300)
}

# Patchwork multipanel
# Make sure list keys exist and follow tax_order
ordered_plots <- perry_rank_plots[tax_order]
ordered_plots <- ordered_plots[!sapply(ordered_plots, is.null)]

# Remove the legend from individual plots; one shared legend on the side
ordered_plots <- map(ordered_plots, function(p) {
  p + theme(legend.position = "none")
})

fig_perry_ranks <- wrap_plots(ordered_plots, ncol = 2, guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Rank of true class in Perry predictions (best variant per outcome)",
    subtitle = "Cell color = row-proportion; cell text = count + (most common predicted instead for misses)",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))
  ) &
  theme(legend.position = "right")

# But this won't actually share the legend if each plot uses geom_tile with
# scale_fill_gradient2 — patchwork's `guides = "collect"` needs identical scales.
# Best approach: remove individual legends and add ONE shared legend separately.

print(fig_perry_ranks)
ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig_perry_ranks_combined.png"),
       fig_perry_ranks, width = 16, height = 16, dpi = 300)
```
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(patchwork)

fig_dir <- here("outputs", "figures_05_02")
thesis_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "top",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA))

tax_order  <- c("taxorder", "taxsuborder", "taxgrtgroup",
                "taxsubgrp_mod1", "taxpartsize", "taxonname_clean")
tax_labels <- c("Order", "Suborder", "Great Group",
                "Subgroup", "Particle Size", "Series")
names(tax_labels) <- tax_order

# ============================================================
# LOAD DATA
# ============================================================
ncss_data <- read.csv(here("outputs", "reports_05_02",
                            "test_metrics_summary_clean.csv")) %>%
  mutate(dataset = "NCSS (internal test)")

# NCSS has reps so we have median + 95% range. For F1 and top-k we use median.
# Compute F1 and top-k summary the same way as kappa
ncss_reps_long <- read.csv(here("outputs", "reports_05_02",
                                 "test_metrics_all_reps_ncss.csv"))

ncss_summary_f1_topk <- ncss_reps_long %>%
  group_by(outcome, engine, space) %>%
  summarise(
    f1_median  = median(f_meas_macro, na.rm = TRUE),
    f1_lo_95   = quantile(f_meas_macro, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    f1_hi_95   = quantile(f_meas_macro, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    topk_median = median(top_k, na.rm = TRUE),
    topk_lo_95  = quantile(top_k, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    topk_hi_95  = quantile(top_k, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ncss_for_plot <- ncss_summary_f1_topk %>%
  mutate(dataset = "NCSS (internal test)")

perry_for_plot <- read.csv(here("outputs", "reports_05_02",
                                 "perry_external_validation.csv")) %>%
  filter(is_min5 | outcome != "taxonname_clean") %>%   # min_n=5 for series
  mutate(
    dataset = "Perry (external)",
    f1_median = f_meas_macro,
    f1_lo_95 = NA_real_,
    f1_hi_95 = NA_real_,
    topk_median = top_k,
    topk_lo_95 = NA_real_,
    topk_hi_95 = NA_real_
  ) %>%
  select(outcome, engine, space, dataset,
         f1_median, f1_lo_95, f1_hi_95,
         topk_median, topk_lo_95, topk_hi_95)

# Combine and pick best per (outcome × dataset) by F1 for the F1 plot
fig_data_f1 <- bind_rows(
  ncss_for_plot %>% select(outcome, engine, space, dataset,
                            value = f1_median, lo = f1_lo_95, hi = f1_hi_95),
  perry_for_plot %>% select(outcome, engine, space, dataset,
                             value = f1_median, lo = f1_lo_95, hi = f1_hi_95)
) %>%
  group_by(outcome, dataset) %>%
  slice_max(value, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    outcome = factor(outcome, levels = tax_order, labels = tax_labels[tax_order]),
    dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("NCSS (internal test)", "Perry (external)")),
    metric = "Macro F1"
  )

# And same for top-k
fig_data_topk <- bind_rows(
  ncss_for_plot %>% select(outcome, engine, space, dataset,
                            value = topk_median, lo = topk_lo_95, hi = topk_hi_95),
  perry_for_plot %>% select(outcome, engine, space, dataset,
                             value = topk_median, lo = topk_lo_95, hi = topk_hi_95)
) %>%
  group_by(outcome, dataset) %>%
  slice_max(value, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    outcome = factor(outcome, levels = tax_order, labels = tax_labels[tax_order]),
    dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("NCSS (internal test)", "Perry (external)")),
    metric = "Top-k Accuracy"
  )

# Combine into one long table for faceted plotting
fig_data_combined <- bind_rows(fig_data_f1, fig_data_topk) %>%
  mutate(metric = factor(metric, levels = c("Macro F1", "Top-k Accuracy")))

# n_classes lookup for k labels
n_classes_lookup <- read.csv(here("outputs", "reports_05_02",
                                   "perry_bundle_inventory.csv")) %>%
  group_by(outcome) %>%
  summarise(n_classes_max = max(n_classes, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(outcome = factor(outcome, levels = tax_order, labels = tax_labels[tax_order]))

n_class_labels <- fig_data_combined %>%
  distinct(outcome, metric) %>%
  left_join(n_classes_lookup, by = "outcome")

# ============================================================
# BUILD THE FIGURE
# ============================================================
fig_f1_topk <- ggplot(fig_data_combined,
                      aes(x = outcome, y = value, fill = dataset)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lo, ymax = hi),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
                width = 0.25, na.rm = TRUE, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", value)),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
            vjust = -0.4, size = 3) +
  geom_text(data = n_class_labels,
            aes(x = outcome, y = 1.06, label = paste0("k=", n_classes_max)),
```
