```{r}
            inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 3, fontface = "italic", color = "grey30") +
  facet_wrap(~ metric, ncol = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("NCSS (internal test)" = "#2C7BB6",
                                "Perry (external)" = "#D7191C"),
                    name = NULL) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.02, 1.10), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL,
       title = "Macro F1 and Top-k accuracy across taxonomic levels",
       subtitle = "NCSS error bars: 95% range across 10 train/test replicates; Perry: canonical-bundle point estimate") +
  thesis_theme +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))

print(fig_f1_topk)
ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig_f1_topk.png"),
       fig_f1_topk, width = 10, height = 9, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
#class comparison:
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(patchwork)

fig_dir <- here("outputs", "figures_05_02")
dir.create(fig_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

thesis_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "top",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA))

tax_order  <- c("taxorder", "taxsuborder", "taxgrtgroup",
                "taxsubgrp_mod1", "taxpartsize", "taxonname_clean")
tax_labels <- c("Order", "Suborder", "Great Group",
                "Subgroup", "Particle Size", "Series")
names(tax_labels) <- tax_order

# ============================================================
# LOAD DATA
# ============================================================
ncss_pack_path <- list.files(here("outputs"),
                              pattern = "^pedon_pack_150_.*\\.csv$",
                              full.names = TRUE)
ncss_pack_path <- ncss_pack_path[which.max(file.mtime(ncss_pack_path))]
ncss <- read.csv(ncss_pack_path)

perry_pack_path <- list.files(here("outputs"),
                               pattern = "^perry_pack_150_.*\\.csv$",
                               full.names = TRUE)
perry_pack_path <- perry_pack_path[which.max(file.mtime(perry_pack_path))]
perry <- read.csv(perry_pack_path)

# Perry truth labels live in perry_fp_clean (not perry_pack itself)
perry_fp_path <- list.files(here("outputs"),
                             pattern = "^perry_fp_clean_.*\\.csv$",
                             full.names = TRUE)
perry_fp_path <- perry_fp_path[which.max(file.mtime(perry_fp_path))]
perry_fp <- read.csv(perry_fp_path)

# Build Perry per-pedon truth labels (one row per pedon, dominant class)
build_perry_truth <- function(perry_fp_clean, truth_col, lowercase = FALSE) {
  if (!truth_col %in% names(perry_fp_clean)) return(tibble())

  dat <- perry_fp_clean %>%
    mutate(val = str_squish(as.character(.data[[truth_col]])),
           val = na_if(val, ""),
           val = na_if(val, "Fill"),
           val = na_if(val, "fill"))

  if (lowercase) dat <- dat %>% mutate(val = str_to_lower(val))
  if (truth_col == "series_clean") dat <- dat %>% mutate(val = str_to_title(val))

  dat %>%
    group_by(Point) %>%
    summarise(
      truth = {
        v <- val[!is.na(val)]
        if (length(v) == 0) NA_character_ else
          names(sort(table(v), decreasing = TRUE))[1]
      },
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(truth)) %>%
    pull(truth)
}

# ============================================================
# COLLECT CLASS DISTRIBUTIONS PER OUTCOME PER DATASET
# ============================================================
get_class_dist <- function(values, outcome_name, dataset_name) {
  values <- values[!is.na(values) & values != ""]
  if (length(values) == 0) return(tibble())
  tibble(
    outcome = outcome_name,
    dataset = dataset_name,
    class = as.character(values)
  ) %>%
    count(outcome, dataset, class, name = "n_pedons") %>%
    group_by(outcome, dataset) %>%
    mutate(pct = 100 * n_pedons / sum(n_pedons)) %>%
    ungroup()
}

# NCSS uses outcome columns directly
ncss_dist <- bind_rows(
  get_class_dist(ncss$taxorder,        "taxorder",        "NCSS"),
  get_class_dist(ncss$taxsuborder,     "taxsuborder",     "NCSS"),
  get_class_dist(ncss$taxgrtgroup,     "taxgrtgroup",     "NCSS"),
  get_class_dist(ncss$taxsubgrp_mod1,  "taxsubgrp_mod1",  "NCSS"),
  get_class_dist(ncss$taxpartsize,     "taxpartsize",     "NCSS"),
  get_class_dist(ncss$taxonname_clean, "taxonname_clean", "NCSS")
)

# Perry truth comes from perry_fp_clean (one row per horizon, dedupe to pedon)
perry_dist <- bind_rows(
  get_class_dist(build_perry_truth(perry_fp, "taxorder"),
                 "taxorder",       "Perry"),
  get_class_dist(build_perry_truth(perry_fp, "taxsuborder"),
                 "taxsuborder",    "Perry"),
  get_class_dist(build_perry_truth(perry_fp, "taxgrtgroup"),
                 "taxgrtgroup",    "Perry"),
  get_class_dist(build_perry_truth(perry_fp, "taxsubgrp_mod1"),
                 "taxsubgrp_mod1", "Perry"),
  get_class_dist(build_perry_truth(perry_fp, "taxpartsize", lowercase = TRUE),
                 "taxpartsize",    "Perry"),
  get_class_dist(build_perry_truth(perry_fp, "series_clean"),
                 "taxonname_clean","Perry")
)

class_dist <- bind_rows(ncss_dist, perry_dist) %>%
  mutate(outcome = factor(outcome, levels = tax_order,
                          labels = tax_labels[tax_order]),
         dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("NCSS", "Perry")))

write.csv(class_dist,
          here("outputs", "reports_05_02", "class_distributions_ncss_perry.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

# ============================================================
# PLOT: ONE PANEL PER OUTCOME, SIDE-BY-SIDE BARS
# ============================================================
plot_class_dist <- function(data, outcome_label, max_classes = 15) {
  d <- data

  # Limit to top classes by combined count if too many (for series)
  if (length(unique(d$class)) > max_classes) {
    top_classes <- d %>%
      group_by(class) %>%
      summarise(total = sum(n_pedons), .groups = "drop") %>%
      slice_max(total, n = max_classes) %>%
      pull(class)
    d <- d %>% filter(class %in% top_classes)
  }

  # Order classes by combined frequency (most common at top)
  class_order <- d %>%
    group_by(class) %>%
    summarise(total = sum(n_pedons), .groups = "drop") %>%
    arrange(total) %>%
    pull(class)

  d <- d %>% mutate(class = factor(class, levels = class_order))

  ggplot(d, aes(x = class, y = pct, fill = dataset)) +
    geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
    geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(pct < 1, "", sprintf("%.0f%%", pct))),
              position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
              hjust = -0.15, size = 2.7) +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("NCSS"  = "#2C7BB6",
                                  "Perry" = "#D7191C"),
                      name = NULL) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18))) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = "Percent of pedons (%)", title = outcome_label) +
    thesis_theme +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
          legend.position = "none")
}

# Different max_classes per outcome
max_classes_per_outcome <- c(
  Order = 7,
  Suborder = 10,
  `Great Group` = 12,
  Subgroup = 12,
  `Particle Size` = 8,
  Series = 15
)

dist_plots <- list()
for (oc in tax_labels) {
  d <- class_dist %>% filter(outcome == oc)
  if (nrow(d) == 0) next
  dist_plots[[oc]] <- plot_class_dist(d, oc,
                                       max_classes = max_classes_per_outcome[[oc]])
}

# Build a "legend-only" plot using one of the data subsets
legend_plot <- ggplot(class_dist %>% filter(outcome == "Order"),
                      aes(x = class, y = pct, fill = dataset)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("NCSS"  = "#2C7BB6",
                                "Perry" = "#D7191C"),
                    name = NULL) +
  thesis_theme +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

shared_legend <- cowplot::get_legend(legend_plot)

# Combine into a multi-panel figure
fig_class_dist <- (
  (dist_plots[["Order"]]      + dist_plots[["Suborder"]]) /
  (dist_plots[["Great Group"]] + dist_plots[["Subgroup"]]) /
  (dist_plots[["Particle Size"]] + dist_plots[["Series"]])
) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Class distributions in NCSS training pool vs Perry validation site",
    subtitle = "Bars show percent of pedons in each class per dataset",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
                  plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, color = "grey30"))
  )

# Add shared legend at top
fig_class_dist_with_legend <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  shared_legend,
  fig_class_dist,
  ncol = 1,
  rel_heights = c(0.05, 1)
)

print(fig_class_dist_with_legend)
ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig_class_distributions.png"),
       fig_class_dist_with_legend, width = 14, height = 16, dpi = 300)
```
```{r}
#density plots of both NCSS and perry for 12 predictors:
# =============================================================================
# fig_eda_density_ncss_perry.R
#
# Density-plot comparison of NCSS vs Perry for 12 key continuous predictors.
# Updated version with:
#   - Outlier-trimmed x-axes (Bt thickness, argillic thickness) using 99th percentile cap
#   - Consistent ordering of panels (texture, color, depth, thickness, indices)
#   - Single shared legend at top
#   - NA handling
# =============================================================================

library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(patchwork)

fig_dir <- here("outputs", "figures_05_02")
dir.create(fig_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Load data
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
ncss_pack_files <- list.files(here("outputs"),
                               pattern = "^pedon_pack_150_.*\\.csv$",
                               full.names = TRUE)
ncss_pack_path <- ncss_pack_files[which.max(file.mtime(ncss_pack_files))]
ncss <- read.csv(ncss_pack_path) %>% mutate(dataset = "NCSS")

perry_pack_files <- list.files(here("outputs"),
                                pattern = "^perry_pack_150_.*\\.csv$",
                                full.names = TRUE)
perry_pack_path <- perry_pack_files[which.max(file.mtime(perry_pack_files))]
perry <- read.csv(perry_pack_path) %>% mutate(dataset = "Perry")

cat("Loaded NCSS:", nrow(ncss), "pedons\n")
cat("Loaded Perry:", nrow(perry), "pedons\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pretty labels for each predictor
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
feat_specs <- tribble(
  ~feature,                ~label,                    ~group,
  "clay_w",                "Clay (%)",                "Texture",
  "sand_w",                "Sand (%)",                "Texture",
  "silt_w",                "Silt (%)",                "Texture",
  "R_w",                   "Red channel (RGB)",       "Color",
  "G_w",                   "Green channel (RGB)",     "Color",
  "B_w",                   "Blue channel (RGB)",      "Color",
  "depth_to_Bt_cm",        "Depth to Bt (cm)",        "Bt/argillic depth",
  "bt_thickness_cm",       "Bt thickness (cm)",       "Bt/argillic depth",
  "max_clay_ratio_0_150",  "Max clay ratio (0-150 cm)",   "Clay translocation",
  "max_clay_delta_0_150",  "Max clay delta (0-150 cm)",   "Clay translocation",
  "depth_to_argillic_cm",  "Depth to argillic (cm)",  "Bt/argillic depth",
  "argillic_thickness_cm", "Argillic thickness (cm)", "Bt/argillic depth"
)

# Stack into long format, filter to feature set
long <- bind_rows(
  ncss  %>% select(dataset, any_of(feat_specs$feature)),
  perry %>% select(dataset, any_of(feat_specs$feature))
) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -dataset, names_to = "feature", values_to = "value") %>%
  filter(!is.na(value), is.finite(value)) %>%
  left_join(feat_specs, by = "feature") %>%
  mutate(label = factor(label, levels = feat_specs$label))

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Manual x-axis caps for depth/thickness features (restricted to 0-150 cm zone)
# Other features get automatic 99th-percentile trimming
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
manual_caps <- tribble(
  ~label,                      ~xmax,
  "Depth to Bt (cm)",          150,
  "Bt thickness (cm)",         150,
  "Depth to argillic (cm)",    150,
  "Argillic thickness (cm)",   150
)

# Auto trim for everything else
auto_trim <- long %>%
  group_by(label) %>%
  summarise(
    p99 = quantile(value, 0.99, na.rm = TRUE),
    p99_5 = quantile(value, 0.995, na.rm = TRUE),
    pmax = max(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    xmax = if_else(pmax > 2 * p99, p99_5, pmax),
    xmax = xmax * 1.05
  ) %>%
  select(label, xmax) %>%
  # Override with manual caps where specified
  anti_join(manual_caps, by = "label") %>%
  bind_rows(manual_caps)

trim_table <- auto_trim

cat("\n--- X-axis trim points per feature ---\n")
print(trim_table)

# Filter long data to within xmax
long_trim <- long %>%
  left_join(trim_table %>% select(label, xmax), by = "label") %>%
  filter(value <= xmax)

cat("\nRows before/after trimming:", nrow(long), "/", nrow(long_trim), "\n")
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Plot
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
thesis_theme <- theme_bw(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", color = NA),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 8)
  )

fig_density <- ggplot(long_trim, aes(x = value, fill = dataset, color = dataset)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.45, linewidth = 0.4) +
  facet_wrap(~ label, scales = "free", ncol = 4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("NCSS"  = "#2C7BB6",
                                "Perry" = "#D7191C")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("NCSS"  = "#1F5780",
                                 "Perry" = "#9A1419"),
                     guide = "none") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Density",
       title = "Predictor distributions: NCSS vs Perry datasets",
       subtitle = "Densities show distributional shift between regional (NCSS) and field-scale (Perry) data") +
  thesis_theme

print(fig_density)

ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig_eda_density_ncss_perry.png"),
       fig_density, width = 14, height = 9, dpi = 300)

cat("\nSaved to:", file.path(fig_dir, "fig_eda_density_ncss_perry.png"), "\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Optional: numeric summary table for inline thesis use
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
summary_tbl <- long %>%
  group_by(label, dataset) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    median = round(median(value, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    q25 = round(quantile(value, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    q75 = round(quantile(value, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    mean = round(mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    sd = round(sd(value, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(label, dataset)

print(summary_tbl, n = Inf)

write.csv(summary_tbl,
          here("outputs", "reports_05_02", "eda_predictor_summary_ncss_perry.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)
```
